title: Open engineering cleanup sprint control surface
description: Create the active backlog and point control-surface docs at the new engineering cleanup sprint so work proceeds under an explicit governed loop.
implementation_tasks: Create the sprint backlog file under docs/internal/
Update WORKING_STATE.md to point at the active backlog and phase
Update AGENTS.md current frontier to reference the sprint backlog and active phase
done_when: The sprint backlog exists with ordered backlog items and test gates
WORKING_STATE.md references the active cleanup sprint backlog
AGENTS.md references the active cleanup sprint backlog and phase

title: Clean obsolete root session artifacts
description: Remove confirmed obsolete root-level reports, screenshots, session artifacts, and stale root backlog JSON files after verifying they are not imported or referenced as live product assets.
implementation_tasks: Reference-audit every deletion candidate with grep/rg before removal
Delete only files confirmed to be obsolete session artifacts
Leave standard repo files and any referenced assets untouched
done_when: Confirmed obsolete session artifacts listed in the sprint scope are removed from the repo root
No deleted file is referenced by live product code or active docs without replacement
The repo root contains only intentional project files and active artifacts

title: Rehome or reconcile root documentation duplicates
description: Move or reconcile important root docs that should not remain in the repo root, specifically DECISIONS.md and any overlapping deployment/setup guides.
implementation_tasks: Move DECISIONS.md to docs/internal/DECISIONS.md
Compare MASTER_SETUP_AND_TESTING_GUIDE.md against docs/MASTER_GUIDE.md and merge any missing useful content into the canonical doc
Compare DEPLOY.md against docs/CICD.md and merge any missing useful content into the canonical doc
Delete root duplicates only after canonical docs contain the retained content
done_when: DECISIONS.md lives under docs/internal/
There is one canonical master setup guide and one canonical deployment/CI doc
Root duplicates are removed only after reconciliation

title: Assess backup directory and retain only intentional backup assets
description: Inspect .backup/ contents, remove only disposable local artifacts if present, and preserve the directory structure because scripts, tests, and docs still reference it.
implementation_tasks: List .backup/ contents
Determine whether any local backup artifacts inside are disposable session remnants
Keep the directory itself because it is part of the supported backup/restore workflow
done_when: .backup/ has been inspected and any disposable junk removed
The supported backup directory structure remains intact

title: Extract intake and classification layers from server/services/incidents.py
description: Read incidents.py fully and split the intake/normalization and classification responsibilities into focused modules without changing public behavior.
implementation_tasks: Read server/services/incidents.py end-to-end
Extract intake/normalization helpers into server/services/intake.py one logical group at a time
Extract SENTINEL classification helpers into server/services/classification.py one logical group at a time
Keep incidents.py behavior identical while imports are rewired incrementally
done_when: Intake/normalization logic lives in server/services/intake.py
Classification logic lives in server/services/classification.py
incidents.py still passes the full baseline with unchanged signatures and return contracts

title: Extract investigation and orchestration layers from server/services/incidents.py
description: Continue the incidents.py split by extracting the PRISM/TRACE/FORGE investigation logic and leaving incidents.py as a thin orchestration layer.
implementation_tasks: Extract PRISM/TRACE/FORGE logic into server/services/investigation.py in small logical groups
Preserve Guardian/audit and handoff contracts while reducing incidents.py to orchestration
Resolve any import or circular dependency issues immediately
done_when: Investigation logic lives in server/services/investigation.py
incidents.py is substantially reduced and functions as orchestration only
All affected tests and full regression gates remain green

title: Extract replay and state layers from server/services/enterprise_runtime.py
description: Read enterprise_runtime.py fully and split replay execution and runtime state management into focused modules without changing runtime behavior.
implementation_tasks: Read server/services/enterprise_runtime.py end-to-end
Extract REPLICA execution logic into server/services/replay.py one logical group at a time
Extract runtime state and queue management into server/services/runtime_state.py one logical group at a time
Keep enterprise_runtime.py as orchestration-only after the split
done_when: Replay execution logic lives in server/services/replay.py
Runtime state management lives in server/services/runtime_state.py
enterprise_runtime.py is reduced to orchestration and all runtime tests still pass

title: Run Python, security, and error-handling audits
description: Work through the Python best-practices, security, and error-handling audits one concern at a time, fixing only real maintainability or correctness issues.
implementation_tasks: Run py_compile/pyflakes/long-line/hardcoded-string scans and fix the highest-impact findings
Run security scans for secrets, SQL formatting, and endpoint validation gaps and fix any real issues
Run error-handling scans for bare excepts, swallowed exceptions, and external-call handling and fix real issues
done_when: High-impact Python audit issues found in scope are fixed
Any real security issues found are fixed
Bare except or silent swallow issues in scope are fixed with explicit handling or logging

title: Run type-hint, dead-code, docstring, dependency, and test-quality audits
description: Complete the remaining audit areas carefully, preserving runtime behavior and avoiding speculative package churn.
implementation_tasks: Add return type hints to public functions in the requested modules where absent
Verify any dead-code candidates with call-site searches before removal
Improve docstrings where the audit finds meaningful gaps
Only touch dependencies if a real issue requires it, and verify Docker/build impacts if changed
Add any needed regression tests without lowering the baseline
done_when: Public type-hint gaps in the requested files are addressed where safe
Any removed dead code was verified unused first
Any dependency or test changes have full verification evidence

title: Close the sprint and refresh control-surface docs
description: After all cleanup and refactor items are complete, refresh the repo control surface, mark the backlog closed, and run the final release gate sequence before merge.
implementation_tasks: Update AGENTS.md, WORKING_STATE.md, and docs/internal/LOOPS_RUNBOOK.md as needed for the new post-sprint state
Mark the sprint backlog completed in WORKING_STATE.md
Run the full final release gate sequence and record the results
done_when: Control-surface docs reflect the finished sprint state
The active backlog is marked complete and no stale frontier remains
The final release gate sequence is green
